toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Command-line entry points for OkGen.

Phase 1 commands:
    okgen compile   Compile xlsx layout definitions -> JSON + validation report.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path

from okgen.detect import detect_layout
from okgen.layout.compiler import compile_dir
from okgen.layout.registry import LayoutRegistry
from okgen.layout.validate import validate_layout
from okgen.okfile import parse_okfile

# Base layout/sample data ships in the repo under data/OkFileDefinitions.
# Override with the OKGEN_DATA_DIR env var to point elsewhere.
_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
DEFAULT_DATA_DIR = os.environ.get(
    "OKGEN_DATA_DIR",
    str(_REPO_ROOT / "data" / "OkFileDefinitions"),
)


def _cmd_compile(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    data_dir = Path(args.data_dir)
    out_dir = Path(args.out)
    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    if not data_dir.is_dir():
        print(f"error: data dir not found: {data_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    layouts = compile_dir(data_dir)
    if not layouts:
        print(f"error: no .xlsx files in {data_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    total_mismatch = 0
    print(f"Compiling layouts from {data_dir}\n")
    for layout in layouts:
        out_path = out_dir / f"{layout.name}.json"
        out_path.write_text(json.dumps(layout.to_dict(), indent=2), encoding="utf-8")

        report = validate_layout(layout)
        total_mismatch += report.mismatch
        flag = "OK " if report.passed else "FAIL"
        print(
            f"[{flag}] {layout.name:<14} "
            f"sections={len(layout.sections)} "
            f"fields ok={report.ok} mismatch={report.mismatch} skipped={report.skipped}"
            f"  -> {out_path}"
        )
        if layout.issues:
            for iss in layout.issues:
                print(f"        layout issue: {iss}")
        for sec in layout.sections:
            for iss in sec.issues:
                print(f"        [{sec.name}] {iss}")
        if args.verbose or not report.passed:
            for c in report.checks:
                if c.status == "mismatch":
                    print(
                        f"        MISMATCH [{c.section}] {c.field} "
                        f"@{c.start} size {c.size}: "
                        f"expected={c.expected!r} actual={c.actual!r}"
                    )
                elif args.verbose and c.status == "skipped":
                    print(f"        skipped  [{c.section}] {c.field}: {c.note}")

    print(f"\nDone. {len(layouts)} layouts compiled to {out_dir}/. "
          f"Total field mismatches: {total_mismatch}.")
    return 0 if total_mismatch == 0 else 1


def _cmd_detect(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    files = [Path(p) for p in args.files]
    missing = [str(p) for p in files if not p.is_file()]
    if missing:
        print(f"error: file(s) not found: {', '.join(missing)}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    any_unmatched = False
    for p in files:
        result = detect_layout(p)
        layout = result.layout or "UNKNOWN"
        any_unmatched = any_unmatched or not result.matched
        print(f"{p.name:<22} -> {layout:<14} ({result.reason})")
        if args.verbose:
            print(f"    marker={result.marker!r} header={result.header[:40]!r}")
    return 0 if not any_unmatched else 1


def _cmd_parse(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    path = Path(args.file)
    if not path.is_file():
        print(f"error: file not found: {path}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 2

    registry = LayoutRegistry.from_dir(args.data_dir)
    okf = parse_okfile(path, registry=registry)

    print(f"{path.name}: layout={okf.layout.name}  records={len(okf.records)}")

    # Byte-exact round-trip check.
    roundtrip_ok = okf.to_bytes() == path.read_bytes()
    print(f"round-trip: {'IDENTICAL' if roundtrip_ok else 'DIFFERS'}")

    print("sections:")
    for name, recs in okf.sections().items():
        sec = recs[0].section if recs and recs[0].section else None
        ignored = f"  (ignored: {', '.join(sec.ignored_fields)})" if sec and sec.ignored_fields else ""
        print(f"  {name:<14} {len(recs)} record(s){ignored}")

    if args.show:
        limit = args.limit if args.limit and args.limit > 0 else None
        for name, recs in okf.sections().items():
            shown = recs if limit is None else recs[:limit]
            print(f"\n[{name}] {len(recs)} record(s):")
            if len(recs) == 1:
                # Single record (e.g. Header): one field per line.
                for fname, val in recs[0].values().items():
                    print(f"    {fname:<22} = {val!r}")
            else:
                # Repeating section: one compact line per record, all records.
                field_names = [f.name for f in recs[0].section.fields] if recs[0].section else []
                for r in shown:
                    pairs = " | ".join(f"{fn}={r.get(fn)!r}" for fn in field_names)
                    print(f"    #{r.index:<4} {pairs}")
                if limit is not None and len(recs) > limit:
                    print(f"    ... ({len(recs) - limit} more; use --limit 0 to show all)")

    return 0 if roundtrip_ok else 1


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="okgen", description=__doc__)
    sub = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)

    c = sub.add_parser("compile", help="Compile xlsx layouts to JSON + validate")
    c.add_argument("--data-dir", default=DEFAULT_DATA_DIR,
                   help=f"dir with *.xlsx layouts (default: {DEFAULT_DATA_DIR})")
    c.add_argument("--out", default="layouts", help="output dir for JSON (default: layouts)")
    c.add_argument("-v", "--verbose", action="store_true", help="show skipped checks too")
    c.set_defaults(func=_cmd_compile)

    d = sub.add_parser("detect", help="Detect which layout an OK file uses")
    d.add_argument("files", nargs="+", help="path(s) to .OK file(s)")
    d.add_argument("-v", "--verbose", action="store_true", help="show marker/header")
    d.set_defaults(func=_cmd_detect)

    p = sub.add_parser("parse", help="Parse an OK file and verify byte-exact round-trip")
    p.add_argument("file", help="path to a .OK file")
    p.add_argument("--data-dir", default=DEFAULT_DATA_DIR,
                   help=f"dir with *.xlsx layouts (default: {DEFAULT_DATA_DIR})")
    p.add_argument("--show", action="store_true", help="print every record's fields per section")
    p.add_argument("--limit", type=int, default=0,
                   help="max records shown per multi-record section (0 = all)")
    p.set_defaults(func=_cmd_parse)

    s = sub.add_parser("serve", help="Run the editor backend (FastAPI)")
    s.add_argument("--host", default="127.0.0.1")
    s.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8000)
    s.add_argument("--data-dir", default=DEFAULT_DATA_DIR)
    s.add_argument("--config-dir", default=None)
    s.set_defaults(func=_cmd_serve)
    return parser


def _cmd_serve(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
    from okgen.web.app import create_app

    app = create_app(data_dir=args.data_dir, config_dir=args.config_dir)
    print(f"OkGen editor on http://{args.host}:{args.port}")
    app.run(host=args.host, port=args.port, debug=args.debug)
    return 0


def main(argv=None) -> int:
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    return args.func(args)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Tests for the backend service + FastAPI endpoints."""

import os
import shutil
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from okgen.api import service
from okgen.config import Config
from okgen.layout.registry import LayoutRegistry

DATA_DIR = Path(
    os.environ.get(
        "OKGEN_DATA_DIR",
        str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "data" / "OkFileDefinitions"),
    )
)

pytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(
    not DATA_DIR.is_dir(), reason=f"sample data dir not present: {DATA_DIR}"
)


@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def registry():
    return LayoutRegistry.from_dir(DATA_DIR)


@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def config():
    return Config.load()


def test_build_tree_only_ok_files(config):
    tree = service.build_tree(DATA_DIR, config)
    assert tree["type"] == "folder"
    files = [c for c in tree["children"] if c["type"] == "file"]
    assert files, "expected .OK files in tree"
    assert all(f["name"].lower().endswith(".ok") for f in files)
    # No .xlsx leaked into the tree.
    assert not any(f["name"].lower().endswith(".xlsx") for f in files)
    # StyleHeader file carries chain 03 -> Homegoods.
    style = next(f for f in files if f["name"] == "StyleHeader.OK")
    assert style["chain"] == "03"
    assert style["chain_info"]["name"] == "Homegoods"
    assert style["layout"] == "StyleHeader"


def test_parse_file_view_shape(registry, config):
    view = service.parse_file_view(DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK", registry, config)
    assert view["layout"] == "StyleHeader"
    assert view["chain"] == "03"
    names = [s["name"] for s in view["sections"]]
    assert "Lane" in names and "Size" in names
    lane = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Lane")
    assert len(lane["records"]) == 10            # all lanes shown
    assert "lane2" in lane["ignored_fields"]      # unsized fields ignored
    # 'indicator' field carries dropdown options from display.yaml.
    header = view["sections"][0]
    ind = next(f for f in header["fields"] if f["name"] == "indicator")
    assert ind["options"] == {"Y": "Yes", "N": "No"}


def test_save_roundtrip_and_edit(tmp_path, registry, config):
    src = DATA_DIR / "CartonLabel.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "CartonLabel.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    original = work.read_bytes()

    # No-op save must be byte-identical.
    res = service.apply_edits(work, [], registry, backup=False)
    assert res["roundtrip_ok"]
    assert work.read_bytes() == original

    # Edit chain in the header (section 0, record 0).
    res = service.apply_edits(
        work,
        [{"section_index": 0, "record_index": 0, "field": "chain", "value": "07"}],
        registry,
        backup=False,
    )
    assert res["edits_applied"] == 1
    view = service.parse_file_view(work, registry, config)
    assert view["sections"][0]["records"][0]["values"]["chain"] == "07"


def test_save_rejects_too_wide_value(tmp_path, registry):
    src = DATA_DIR / "CartonLabel.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "CartonLabel.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    with pytest.raises(service.EditError):
        service.apply_edits(
            work,
            [{"section_index": 0, "record_index": 0, "field": "chain", "value": "123"}],
            registry,
            backup=False,
        )


def test_save_as_and_copy_delete(tmp_path, registry):
    src = DATA_DIR / "DistLabels.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "DistLabels.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)

    # Save As to a new path.
    other = tmp_path / "DistLabels_copy.OK"
    service.apply_edits(work, [], registry, target_path=str(other), backup=False)
    assert other.exists()

    # Copy + delete file ops.
    dst = tmp_path / "DistLabels_copy2.OK"
    service.copy_file(work, dst)
    assert dst.exists()
    service.delete_file(dst)
    assert not dst.exists()


def test_fastapi_endpoints():
    from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

    from okgen.api.app import create_app

    client = TestClient(create_app(data_dir=DATA_DIR))
    assert client.get("/api/health").json()["ok"] is True
    chains = client.get("/api/chains").json()
    assert chains["03"]["name"] == "Homegoods"
    parsed = client.get("/api/parse", params={"path": str(DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK")}).json()
    assert parsed["layout"] == "StyleHeader"

---
name: okgen-project-goal
description: "OkGen project goal, data format, and chosen tech stack for the OK-file visualizer/editor"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 180cda22-7299-44d8-8a0c-d82b4ae2d316
---

**Goal:** Build a visualizer/editor for fixed-width `.OK` data files. When a user opens any OK file, the app auto-detects which `.xlsx` layout defines it, parses the fixed-width records, and renders editable forms so the user edits field *values* only — the program owns character position/size/padding. Scale target: 100s of files. The user's core pain is manually tracking field positions/sizes.

**Data location:** `/Users/praveendx/repos/OkGenData/OkFileDefinitions` (paired `*.OK` data files + `TJXNA_*Layout.xlsx` definitions). This is OUTSIDE the OkGen repo.

**OK file format:** fixed-width records, one per line. First char = record marker (`|`=header, `#`=detail); marker is stripped, then fields slice by 1-based position+size. Line ends with `\` terminator, space-padded to fixed width. Likely latin-1/cp1252 encoding (non-ASCII bytes present).

**xlsx layout format:** one tab per OK-file section (Header, Store, Detail, Lane, Size, TSticker). In each tab: row1 = real sample record (marker stripped), row2 = column headers (field_name, field_size, output_field_name, Position, Value, field_type, field_name_section...), rows3+ = field defs. Row1 sample + per-field `Value` column = built-in validation (slice sample, assert == Value).

**Chosen stack:** Local web app — FastAPI (Python) backend wrapping the parser/serializer core + React frontend. Decided 2026-06-20.

**Layout detection rule (RESOLVED):** Decided from the OK header (first) line, in RAW positions (1-based, INCLUDING the leading marker char; raw position = xlsx Position + 1): raw pos4=='N'→StyleHeader; raw pos4=='Y'→Preticket; raw pos4 in ('7','9')→DistLabels; raw pos5..6=='C:'→CartonLabel. Implemented in `src/okgen/detect.py`, verified on all 4 samples.

**Lane handling (RESOLVED):** Ignore the NULL-size lane2–lane10 rows in StyleHeader; lane1's position/size applies to all lane records (Lane is a repeating record using the lane1 field def). Same idea for `*_totqty` aggregates — skip for now.

**Key xlsx columns to use:** `Position`, `output_field_name`, `field_size`.

**Still open:** per-field padding/justification rules for byte-exact save (infer numeric=zero-pad, char=space-pad, validate against samples). xlsx has dirty rows (#VALUE!, NULL) — Position is formula-computed/cumulative; compiler recomputes start from cumulative sizes (matches declared Position where valid; 0 sample mismatches).

**Record/section model (RESOLVED in Phase 2):** Markers map to sections — line 0 = Header (strip 1 marker char, `|` or `\xa6`); detail lines with `#`/`&` strip 1 char and map to non-header sections in order of first appearance (`#`→first detail, `&`→second); detail lines with no marker (Preticket Detail, alphanumeric first char) have offset 0 → first detail section. Lines end with `\` terminator + space padding + `\r\n`. Files read/written as Latin-1.

**Phase 1 status (DONE):** layout compiler + validator + detector under `src/okgen/`, venv at `.venv`. Run: `.venv/bin/okgen compile`, `.venv/bin/okgen detect <file>`.

**Phase 2 status (DONE):** parser/serializer in `src/okgen/okfile.py` with byte-exact round-trip (raw-preserving Record; fields are overlaid views; edits only overwrite their span). `okgen parse <file> [--show]`. LayoutRegistry in `src/okgen/layout/registry.py`. All 4 files round-trip byte-identical; 20 tests passing. NOTE: `_fit` padding/justification is inferred from sample Value (numeric-zeropad→right/'0', else left/space) — heuristic, may need real per-field rules before production editing.

**TARGET OS: Windows** (NOT Mac — user confirmed). Engine is cross-platform: reads/writes bytes (CRLF preserved, no newline translation), uses pathlib. venv activates via `.venv\Scripts\activate` on Windows.

**Phase 3 = FastAPI + React webapp** (user chose web over desktop despite recommendation; solved local-file friction by having the local FastAPI backend do file I/O by path). Requirements: VSCode-style left tree showing ONLY .OK files; per-chain file icons (01=TJMAXX, 02=Marshalls, 03=Homegoods, 04=Winners, 06=HomeSense); central editor with fields grouped by section; coded values shown as friendly strings via user-editable config; width validation from xls field_size; save writes values back at fixed positions byte-exact; file actions Save As/Delete/Copy/Paste.

**Phase 3a status (DONE):** config system in `src/okgen/config.py` + `config/chains.yaml` (chain→brand+color+icon) + `config/display.yaml` (rules: match chain/layout/format/field → {code:label}, most-specific wins). `read_chain(path)` in detect.py for tree icons. PyYAML dep added.

**UI decisions:** coded fields → DROPDOWN of labels (writes the code); Save As/Delete/Copy/Paste operate on FILES in the tree (VSCode-style, not records).

**Phase 3b status (DONE):** FastAPI backend. `src/okgen/api/service.py` (pure, testable): build_tree (only .OK files, pruned, per-file chain+layout for icons), parse_file_view (sections→fields w/ size+dropdown options+all records), apply_edits (width-validated, byte-exact, .bak backup, Save As via target_path), delete/copy/rename file ops. `src/okgen/api/app.py` FastAPI wrapper; `okgen serve` CLI. 32 tests passing. Live server verified. Next: 3c React frontend (Vite + React): tree w/ chain badges, section editor w/ dropdowns + width-limited inputs, file actions.

See [[okgen-commit-to-main]] for the repo's git workflow.

content.text:
## Phase 3c complete — the full editor, 100% Python (Flask) ✅

No Node, no build step. The same JSON API stays as a clean seam if you ever move to React.

### How to run it
```bash
cd /Users/praveendx/repos/OkGen
source .venv/bin/activate
okgen serve
```
Then open **http://127.0.0.1:8000** in your browser.

1. Paste a folder path into the top bar (e.g. `/Users/praveendx/Downloads/Homegoods`) → **Open Folder**.
2. The left tree shows **only `.OK` files**, each with a **colored chain badge** (TJX/MAR/HG/WIN/HS).
3. Click a file → the center pane shows all fields **grouped by section** (Header as a form; Lane/Size/etc. as tables with every record).
4. **Coded fields are dropdowns** (e.g. `indicator` → Yes/No); plain fields are **width-limited inputs** (can't exceed `field_size`).
5. Edit → cells turn amber (dirty) → **Save** (writes byte-exact, `.bak` backup) or **Save As…**.
6. **Right-click a file** in the tree → Copy / Paste here / Rename / Delete.

### What I verified
- Flask serves the HTML shell + JS/CSS + all `/api/*` endpoints (live-tested).
- `/api/parse` returns correct sections, dropdown options, all records, `roundtrip_ok: true`.
- **33 tests pass**, including Flask endpoint + save tests.

### Honest caveats
- I verified the **server and API** thoroughly, but I can't *see* the rendered page — the visual layout/interactions are unverified by me. You'll want to click through it. If anything looks off (styling, a control, an action), tell me and I'll fix it.
- It's a local dev server (Flask's built-in). Fine for your use; for a packaged Windows deploy we'd later add a production server (e.g. `waitress`, which is pure-Python and Windows-friendly).

Go ahead and open it in the browser and try your Homegoods folder. Once you've confirmed it works for you, we'll checkpoint it (I'd tag `phase3c-flask-ui`). Want me to also add a quick `okgen open <folder>` that launches the server and opens your browser automatically?

message.content: Where is the config file for the strings.  Also, I see the format is not editable, it is a 1chr length. How can I edit it.

content.text:
Both string config files live in the repo at:
```
/Users/praveendx/repos/OkGen/config/
├── chains.yaml     ← chain code → brand name + icon color (01→TJMAXX, etc.)
└── display.yaml    ← coded field values → friendly strings (the dropdowns)
```

## Why `format` isn't free-text
You chose **dropdowns for coded fields**, and `format` currently *is* a coded field because of a rule I seeded in `config/display.yaml`:

```yaml
  - match: {layout: StyleHeader, field: format}
    values:
      A: "Format A"
      B: "Format B"

  - match: {chain: "03", layout: StyleHeader, format: A, field: format}
    values:
      A: "Regular Tag (Homegoods)"
```

So for StyleHeader files, `format` shows as a dropdown limited to **A / B**. That's why you can't type a new value — the dropdown only offers what's configured.

## Two ways to edit `format`

**Option A — keep it a dropdown, but add your real format codes** (recommended, since these are a fixed set):
```yaml
  - match: {layout: StyleHeader, field: format}
    values:
      A: "Format A"
      B: "Format B"
      L: "Format L"
      "6": "6-Digit"
```

**Option B — make it a plain typed box** (1-char, since `field_size` is 1): just **delete/comment out** the two `format` rules above. With no mapping, `format` becomes a normal width-limited text input.

## Important: reload after editing config
The app loads `config/*.yaml` at startup, so after you edit either file, **restart the server** (Ctrl-C, then `okgen serve` again) to see the change. (Or run `okgen serve --debug` so it reloads automatically.)

---

Tell me which you want and I'll set it up:
- The **full list of valid `format` codes** per chain/layout (I'll wire them into the dropdown), **or**
- Just make `format` free-text for now.

And the same question applies to any other coded field — give me the value→label sets and I'll fill out `display.yaml` properly.

message.content: The full list of valid format codes per chain/layout.

content.text:
Great — I'll wire those into `config/display.yaml`. I just need the actual codes from you, since only you have them. Here's a fill-in template — replace the examples with your real values and send it back. Add or remove rows freely:

```yaml
# Format codes per layout (and per chain if they differ).
# Give me: layout, the chain (or "all" if same for every chain),
#          and each format code -> what it means.

StyleHeader:
  all:                      # or list specific chains if they differ
    A: "Format A – Regular Tag"
    B: "Format B – ?"
    # ...

Preticket:
  all:
    L: "?"
    # ...

CartonLabel:
  all:
    "1": "?"
    "2": "?"
    # ...

DistLabels:
  all:
    "7": "?"
    "9": "?"
    # ...
```

A few clarifying questions so I structure it right:

1. **Do format codes differ by chain?** e.g. is StyleHeader format `A` the same meaning for TJMAXX (01) and Homegoods (03), or chain-specific? If they're the same across chains, use `all`. If some differ, tell me which chain overrides which code.

2. **Quote numeric codes** (like `1`, `7`) so YAML treats them as the literal characters — I'll handle that, just give me the raw values.

3. While you're at it, if there are **other coded fields** besides `format` (e.g. `type`, `comp_up`, `indicator`, anything chain/format-specific), list those too and I'll add them in the same pass.

Send the filled-in list and I'll update `display.yaml`, restart-test it, and you'll see the dropdowns populate.

Format of your reply can be as loose as: *"StyleHeader, all chains: A=Regular Tag, B=Sticker, L=Lane Label"* — I'll convert it to the YAML.

lastPrompt: The full list of valid format codes per chain/layout.

Also, open folder should open be easy for the user in windows it should open the file explorer where he can browse the folder he wants ( much like upload buttons on any other application).  The maximum number of rows for Lanes is only 10 the add button should be disabled when that row count is reached.

file.content:
path,
    edits: List[dict],
    registry: LayoutRegistry,
    target_path=None,
    backup: bool = True,
) -> dict:
    """Apply field edits and write the file (byte-exact for untouched spans).

    ``edits``: list of {section_index, record_index, field, value}.
    ``target_path``: if given, writes there (Save As); else overwrites ``path``.
    Validates each value against its field width before writing anything.
    """
    src = Path(path)
    okf = parse_okfile(src, registry=registry)
    by_index = {r.index: r for r in okf.records}

    errors: List[dict] = []
    for e in edits:
        rec = by_index.get(e["record_index"])
        if rec is None:
            errors.append({"edit": e, "error": "record_index out of range"})
            continue
        field = e["field"]
        value = e["value"]
        try:
            f = rec._field(field)  # noqa: SLF001 — internal lookup
        except KeyError as exc:
            errors.append({"edit": e, "error": str(exc)})
            continue
        if f.size is not None and len(value) > f.size:
            errors.append({
                "edit": e,
                "error": f"value '{value}' exceeds field '{field}' size {f.size}",
            })

    if errors:
        raise EditError(str(errors))

    # All valid — apply.
    for e in edits:
        by_index[e["record_index"]].set(e["field"], e["value"])

    out = Path(target_path) if target_path else src
    if backup and out.exists() and target_path is None:
        shutil.copy2(out, out.with_suffix(out.suffix + ".bak"))
    okf.save(out)

    return {
        "path": str(out),
        "written": True,
        "edits_applied": len(edits),
        "roundtrip_ok": okf.to_bytes() == out.read_bytes(),
    }


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# File operations (tree actions)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
def delete_file(path) -> dict:
    p = Path(path)

    }


def _apply_edits_to_okf(okf, edits: List[dict]) -> None:
    """Validate field widths, then apply edits in place. Raises EditError."""
    by_index = {r.index: r for r in okf.records}
    errors: List[dict] = []
    for e in edits:
        rec = by_index.get(e["record_index"])
        if rec is None:
            errors.append({"edit": e, "error": "record_index out of range"})
            continue
        try:
            f = rec._field(e["field"])  # noqa: SLF001 — internal lookup
        except KeyError as exc:
            errors.append({"edit": e, "error": str(exc)})
            continue
        if f.size is not None and len(e["value"]) > f.size:
            errors.append({
                "edit": e,
                "error": f"value '{e['value']}' exceeds field '{e['field']}' size {f.size}",
            })
    if errors:
        raise EditError(str(errors))
    for e in edits:
        by_index[e["record_index"]].set(e["field"], e["value"])


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Add a record to a repeating section
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
def add_record(
    path,
    section_index: int,
    edits: List[dict],
    registry: LayoutRegistry,
    config: Config,
    backup: bool = True,
) -> dict:
    """Apply pending edits, append a blank record to a section, and save.

    Enforces the section's ``max_records`` limit. The new record clones the
    structure (marker, terminator, padding, line ending) of an existing record
    in the section and blanks its field spans, so the file stays well-formed.
    """
    from okgen.okfile import Record

    src = Path(path)
    okf = parse_okfile(src, registry=registry)
    _apply_edits_to_okf(okf, edits)

    grouped = list(okf.sections().items())
    if section_index < 0 or section_index >= len(grouped):
        raise EditError(f"section_index {section_index} out of range")
    sec_name, recs = grouped[section_index]
    if not recs or recs[0].section is None:
        raise EditError(f"section '{sec_name}' has no template record to clone")

    limit = config.max_records(okf.layout.name, sec_name)
    if limit is not None and len(recs) >= limit:
        raise EditError(f"section '{sec_name}' is at its limit of {limit} records")

    template = recs[-1]
    blank = Record(
        raw=_blank_content(template),
        offset=template.offset,
        section=template.section,
        index=template.index,  # placeholder; reassigned on reload
    )
    insert_at = okf.records.index(template) + 1
    okf.records.insert(insert_at, blank)
    _normalize_eols(okf)

    out = src
    if backup and out.exists():
        shutil.copy2(out, out.with_suffix(out.suffix + ".bak"))
    okf.save(out)

    view = parse_file_view(out, registry, config)
    view["added_section"] = sec_name
    return view


def _eol_of(okf) -> str:
    """The per-line terminator that precedes the '\\n' separator ('\\r' or '')."""
    return "\r" if any(r.raw.endswith("\r") for r in okf.records) else ""


def _blank_content(record) -> str:
    """Clone a record's structure but blank every field span (no trailing EOL)."""
    content = record.raw.rstrip("\r")
    chars = list(content)
    if record.section:
        for f in record.section.fields:
            if f.start is None or f.size is None:
                continue
            start = record.offset + f.start - 1
            for i in range(start, min(start + f.size, len(chars))):
                chars[i] = " "
    return "".join(chars)


def _normalize_eols(okf) -> None:
    """Re-apply consistent line endings after an insert.

    Interior lines (and the last line when the file ends with a newline) carry
    the EOL; an unterminated final line does not. Idempotent on well-formed
    files, so it only changes bytes around an inserted record.
    """
    eol = _eol_of(okf)
    n = len(okf.records)
    for i, rec in enumerate(okf.records):
        content = rec.raw.rstrip("\r")
        is_last = i == n - 1
        if is_last and not okf.trailing_newline:
            rec.raw = content
        else:
            rec.raw = content + eol


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# Native folder picker (local app convenience)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
def browse_folder(initial: Optional[str] = None) -> dict:
    """Open the OS folder-chooser on the local machine and return the choice.

    Runs in a subprocess so Tk never touches the Flask server thread. Works on
    Windows/macOS desktop sessions; returns {"path": None} if cancelled or if
    no GUI is available.
    """
    import subprocess
    import sys

    script = (
        "import tkinter as tk\n"
        "from tkinter import filedialog\n"
        "r = tk.Tk(); r.withdraw(); r.attributes('-topmost', True)\n"
        "print(filedialog.askdirectory() or '')\n"
    )
    try:
        proc = subprocess.run(
            [sys.executable, "-c", script],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=300,
        )
        chosen = proc.stdout.strip()
        return {"path": chosen or None}
    except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - depends on desktop session
        return {"path": None, "error": str(exc)}

input.new_string:
def test_max_records_in_view(registry, config):
    view = service.parse_file_view(DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK", registry, config)
    lane = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Lane")
    size = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Size")
    assert lane["max_records"] == 10        # configured limit
    assert size["max_records"] is None      # no limit


def test_add_record_to_unlimited_section(tmp_path, registry, config):
    src = DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "StyleHeader.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    before = service.parse_file_view(work, registry, config)
    size_idx = next(s["index"] for s in before["sections"] if s["name"] == "Size")
    n_before = len(next(s for s in before["sections"] if s["name"] == "Size")["records"])

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Tests for the backend service + FastAPI endpoints."""

import os
import shutil
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from okgen.api import service
from okgen.config import Config
from okgen.layout.registry import LayoutRegistry

DATA_DIR = Path(
    os.environ.get(
        "OKGEN_DATA_DIR",
        str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "data" / "OkFileDefinitions"),
    )
)

pytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(
    not DATA_DIR.is_dir(), reason=f"sample data dir not present: {DATA_DIR}"
)


@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def registry():
    return LayoutRegistry.from_dir(DATA_DIR)


@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def config():
    return Config.load()


def test_build_tree_only_ok_files(config):
    tree = service.build_tree(DATA_DIR, config)
    assert tree["type"] == "folder"
    files = [c for c in tree["children"] if c["type"] == "file"]
    assert files, "expected .OK files in tree"
    assert all(f["name"].lower().endswith(".ok") for f in files)
    # No .xlsx leaked into the tree.
    assert not any(f["name"].lower().endswith(".xlsx") for f in files)
    # StyleHeader file carries chain 03 -> Homegoods.
    style = next(f for f in files if f["name"] == "StyleHeader.OK")
    assert style["chain"] == "03"
    assert style["chain_info"]["name"] == "Homegoods"
    assert style["layout"] == "StyleHeader"


def test_parse_file_view_shape(registry, config):
    view = service.parse_file_view(DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK", registry, config)
    assert view["layout"] == "StyleHeader"
    assert view["chain"] == "03"
    names = [s["name"] for s in view["sections"]]
    assert "Lane" in names and "Size" in names
    lane = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Lane")
    assert len(lane["records"]) == 10            # all lanes shown
    assert "lane2" in lane["ignored_fields"]      # unsized fields ignored
    # 'indicator' field carries dropdown options from display.yaml.
    header = view["sections"][0]
    ind = next(f for f in header["fields"] if f["name"] == "indicator")
    assert ind["options"] == {"Y": "Yes", "N": "No"}


def test_save_roundtrip_and_edit(tmp_path, registry, config):
    src = DATA_DIR / "CartonLabel.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "CartonLabel.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    original = work.read_bytes()

    # No-op save must be byte-identical.
    res = service.apply_edits(work, [], registry, backup=False)
    assert res["roundtrip_ok"]
    assert work.read_bytes() == original

    # Edit chain in the header (section 0, record 0).
    res = service.apply_edits(
        work,
        [{"section_index": 0, "record_index": 0, "field": "chain", "value": "07"}],
        registry,
        backup=False,
    )
    assert res["edits_applied"] == 1
    view = service.parse_file_view(work, registry, config)
    assert view["sections"][0]["records"][0]["values"]["chain"] == "07"


def test_save_rejects_too_wide_value(tmp_path, registry):
    src = DATA_DIR / "CartonLabel.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "CartonLabel.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    with pytest.raises(service.EditError):
        service.apply_edits(
            work,
            [{"section_index": 0, "record_index": 0, "field": "chain", "value": "123"}],
            registry,
            backup=False,
        )


def test_save_as_and_copy_delete(tmp_path, registry):
    src = DATA_DIR / "DistLabels.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "DistLabels.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)

    # Save As to a new path.
    other = tmp_path / "DistLabels_copy.OK"
    service.apply_edits(work, [], registry, target_path=str(other), backup=False)
    assert other.exists()

    # Copy + delete file ops.
    dst = tmp_path / "DistLabels_copy2.OK"
    service.copy_file(work, dst)
    assert dst.exists()
    service.delete_file(dst)
    assert not dst.exists()


def test_flask_endpoints():
    from okgen.web.app import create_app

    app = create_app(data_dir=DATA_DIR)
    client = app.test_client()
    assert client.get("/api/health").get_json()["ok"] is True
    chains = client.get("/api/chains").get_json()
    assert chains["03"]["name"] == "Homegoods"
    parsed = client.get(
        "/api/parse", query_string={"path": str(DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK")}
    ).get_json()
    assert parsed["layout"] == "StyleHeader"
    # The HTML UI shell renders.
    assert client.get("/").status_code == 200


def test_flask_save_endpoint(tmp_path):
    import shutil as _sh

    from okgen.web.app import create_app

    src = DATA_DIR / "CartonLabel.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "CartonLabel.OK"
    _sh.copy2(src, work)
    client = create_app(data_dir=DATA_DIR).test_client()
    res = client.post("/api/save", json={
        "path": str(work),
        "edits": [{"section_index": 0, "record_index": 0, "field": "chain", "value": "07"}],
        "backup": False,
    })
    assert res.status_code == 200
    assert res.get_json()["edits_applied"] == 1

---
name: okgen-project-goal
description: "OkGen project goal, data format, and chosen tech stack for the OK-file visualizer/editor"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 180cda22-7299-44d8-8a0c-d82b4ae2d316
---

**Goal:** Build a visualizer/editor for fixed-width `.OK` data files. When a user opens any OK file, the app auto-detects which `.xlsx` layout defines it, parses the fixed-width records, and renders editable forms so the user edits field *values* only — the program owns character position/size/padding. Scale target: 100s of files. The user's core pain is manually tracking field positions/sizes.

**Data location:** `/Users/praveendx/repos/OkGenData/OkFileDefinitions` (paired `*.OK` data files + `TJXNA_*Layout.xlsx` definitions). This is OUTSIDE the OkGen repo.

**OK file format:** fixed-width records, one per line. First char = record marker (`|`=header, `#`=detail); marker is stripped, then fields slice by 1-based position+size. Line ends with `\` terminator, space-padded to fixed width. Likely latin-1/cp1252 encoding (non-ASCII bytes present).

**xlsx layout format:** one tab per OK-file section (Header, Store, Detail, Lane, Size, TSticker). In each tab: row1 = real sample record (marker stripped), row2 = column headers (field_name, field_size, output_field_name, Position, Value, field_type, field_name_section...), rows3+ = field defs. Row1 sample + per-field `Value` column = built-in validation (slice sample, assert == Value).

**Chosen stack:** Local web app — FastAPI (Python) backend wrapping the parser/serializer core + React frontend. Decided 2026-06-20.

**Layout detection rule (RESOLVED):** Decided from the OK header (first) line, in RAW positions (1-based, INCLUDING the leading marker char; raw position = xlsx Position + 1): raw pos4=='N'→StyleHeader; raw pos4=='Y'→Preticket; raw pos4 in ('7','9')→DistLabels; raw pos5..6=='C:'→CartonLabel. Implemented in `src/okgen/detect.py`, verified on all 4 samples.

**Lane handling (RESOLVED):** Ignore the NULL-size lane2–lane10 rows in StyleHeader; lane1's position/size applies to all lane records (Lane is a repeating record using the lane1 field def). Same idea for `*_totqty` aggregates — skip for now.

**Key xlsx columns to use:** `Position`, `output_field_name`, `field_size`.

**Still open:** per-field padding/justification rules for byte-exact save (infer numeric=zero-pad, char=space-pad, validate against samples). xlsx has dirty rows (#VALUE!, NULL) — Position is formula-computed/cumulative; compiler recomputes start from cumulative sizes (matches declared Position where valid; 0 sample mismatches).

**Record/section model (RESOLVED in Phase 2):** Markers map to sections — line 0 = Header (strip 1 marker char, `|` or `\xa6`); detail lines with `#`/`&` strip 1 char and map to non-header sections in order of first appearance (`#`→first detail, `&`→second); detail lines with no marker (Preticket Detail, alphanumeric first char) have offset 0 → first detail section. Lines end with `\` terminator + space padding + `\r\n`. Files read/written as Latin-1.

**Phase 1 status (DONE):** layout compiler + validator + detector under `src/okgen/`, venv at `.venv`. Run: `.venv/bin/okgen compile`, `.venv/bin/okgen detect <file>`.

**Phase 2 status (DONE):** parser/serializer in `src/okgen/okfile.py` with byte-exact round-trip (raw-preserving Record; fields are overlaid views; edits only overwrite their span). `okgen parse <file> [--show]`. LayoutRegistry in `src/okgen/layout/registry.py`. All 4 files round-trip byte-identical; 20 tests passing. NOTE: `_fit` padding/justification is inferred from sample Value (numeric-zeropad→right/'0', else left/space) — heuristic, may need real per-field rules before production editing.

**TARGET OS: Windows** (NOT Mac — user confirmed). Engine is cross-platform: reads/writes bytes (CRLF preserved, no newline translation), uses pathlib. venv activates via `.venv\Scripts\activate` on Windows.

**Phase 3 = FastAPI + React webapp** (user chose web over desktop despite recommendation; solved local-file friction by having the local FastAPI backend do file I/O by path). Requirements: VSCode-style left tree showing ONLY .OK files; per-chain file icons (01=TJMAXX, 02=Marshalls, 03=Homegoods, 04=Winners, 06=HomeSense); central editor with fields grouped by section; coded values shown as friendly strings via user-editable config; width validation from xls field_size; save writes values back at fixed positions byte-exact; file actions Save As/Delete/Copy/Paste.

**Phase 3a status (DONE):** config system in `src/okgen/config.py` + `config/chains.yaml` (chain→brand+color+icon) + `config/display.yaml` (rules: match chain/layout/format/field → {code:label}, most-specific wins). `read_chain(path)` in detect.py for tree icons. PyYAML dep added.

**UI decisions:** coded fields → DROPDOWN of labels (writes the code); Save As/Delete/Copy/Paste operate on FILES in the tree (VSCode-style, not records).

**Phase 3b status (DONE):** service layer `src/okgen/api/service.py` (pure, framework-agnostic, REUSED by Flask): build_tree (only .OK files, pruned, per-file chain+layout for icons), parse_file_view (sections→fields w/ size+dropdown options+all records), apply_edits (width-validated, byte-exact, .bak backup, Save As via target_path), delete/copy/rename file ops.

**STACK PIVOT (user decision):** dropped FastAPI/React → building COMPLETELY IN PYTHON with **Flask** (server-rendered HTML + vanilla JS), keeping the JSON API as the seam to migrate to React later if needed. service.py unchanged.

**Phase 3c status (DONE):** Flask app `src/okgen/web/app.py` (create_app; serves `/` HTML UI + `/api/*` JSON: health, chains, tree, parse, save, file/delete|copy|rename). UI in `src/okgen/web/templates/index.html` + `static/app.js` (~vanilla SPA: VSCode-style tree w/ chain badges, header form + repeating-section tables, dropdowns for coded fields, maxLength=field_size width limits, dirty tracking, Save/Save As, right-click Copy/Paste/Delete/Rename) + `static/styles.css` (dark theme). `okgen serve` runs Flask. Deps: Flask (removed fastapi/uvicorn/httpx). 33 tests passing; live server verified (HTML+static+API). Run: `okgen serve` → http://127.0.0.1:8000.

See [[okgen-commit-to-main]] for the repo's git workflow.

UI in `src/okgen/web/templates/index.html` + `static/app.js` (vanilla SPA: VSCode-style tree w/ chain badges, header form + repeating-section tables, dropdowns for coded fields, maxLength=field_size width limits, dirty tracking, Save/Save As, right-click Copy/Paste/Delete/Rename) + `static/styles.css` (dark theme). `okgen serve` runs Flask. Deps: Flask. Run: `okgen serve` → http://127.0.0.1:8000.

+**Phase 3d status (DONE):** (1) Add-row buttons for repeating sections via POST `/api/record/add` (clones a record's structure, blanks fields, byte-exact; applies pending edits first then saves). (2) Per-section row limits in `config/limits.yaml` (StyleHeader.Lane=10); Add disabled at limit, backend returns 422. (3) Unsaved-changes asterisk `*` on file title + tree node; confirm-discard guard when switching files/folders + beforeunload guard. (4) Native OS folder picker via POST `/api/browse-folder` (subprocess tkinter askdirectory; works on Windows/Mac desktop, returns path null if headless/cancelled). 37 tests passing; live-verified. NOTE/OPEN: adding a Lane/Size row does NOT auto-update header count fields (lane_rec/size_rec) — user edits those manually unless they ask for auto-update.
See [[okgen-commit-to-main]] for the repo's git workflow.

content.text:
All five requests are built and verified:

## What's new
1. **Add-row buttons** on every repeating section (Lane, Size, Store, Detail) — top-right of each section. Clicking adds a blank record (cloned structure, byte-exact), applying any pending edits first, then saves and reloads.
2. **Lane capped at 10** — the button shows `10 / 10 record(s)`, is **disabled** at the limit, and the backend also rejects it (422). The limit lives in `config/limits.yaml`, so you can add limits for other sections later.
3. **Unsaved asterisk `*`** — appears on the file title *and* the tree node while you have unsaved edits.
4. **Navigation guard** — switching files, opening another folder, or closing the tab with unsaved edits prompts "Discard unsaved changes?" So you won't silently lose work. (You can move to another file — it just asks first.)
5. **Native folder picker** — **Open Folder…** now opens the OS folder dialog (Windows Explorer / Mac Finder), like an upload button. The text box remains as a manual paste-a-path fallback.

## How to test
```bash
okgen serve         # then open http://127.0.0.1:8000
```
Open Folder → pick a folder → click a file → try Add row, edit a cell (watch the `*` appear), Save, switch files (watch the guard).

## Two things to flag
- **Add saves immediately** (it writes the blank row to disk, with a `.bak`). That's why it also commits any pending edits in the same step — no data loss.
- **Header count fields aren't auto-updated.** Adding a Lane/Size row does **not** change the header's `lane_rec` / `size_rec` count fields — you'd edit those yourself. If you want me to auto-sync those counts when rows are added/removed, say so and I'll wire it up (I just need to know which header field tracks which section).

The **native dialog** and the **visual UI** I can't see from here — please click through them. Once it looks right, tell me and I'll checkpoint (tag `phase3d-addrows-folderpicker`).

content: Goal: build a Python/Flask editor for fixed-width OK files. Just finished adding row-add buttons (Lane capped at 10), unsaved-change asterisks, and a native folder picker; all 37 tests pass. Next: you test the UI in the browser, then I checkpoint it.

# Display-label config: show coded field values as friendly strings.
#
# Each rule maps a field's raw codes to labels. A rule applies to a file when
# its `match` criteria fit. Omit a criterion (or set it to "*") to match any
# value. When several rules match the same field, the MOST SPECIFIC one wins
# (the one matching the most of chain/layout/format).
#
# match keys:
#   chain   2-char chain code ("01".."06"), or "*"  (any)
#   layout  layout name (CartonLabel | DistLabels | Preticket | StyleHeader), or "*"
#   format  the `format` field value (e.g. A, B, L, 1, 7), or "*"
#   field   REQUIRED — the output_field_name this rule applies to
#
# values: { raw_code: "Label shown to user", ... }
#
# Notes:
#   - The `chain` field itself is labeled from chains.yaml; you don't need a
#     rule for it here (but one here would override).
#   - In the editor, a field that has a values map is shown as a dropdown of
#     these labels; saving writes the underlying code at the fixed position.
rules:
  # ---- generic (any chain/layout/format) ----
  - match: {field: indicator}
    values: {Y: "Yes", N: "No"}

  - match: {field: psi}
    values: {Y: "Yes", N: "No"}

  - match: {field: prflag}
    values: {Y: "Yes", N: "No"}

  - match: {field: comp_up}
    values: {Y: "Compare-at & Up", N: "Standard"}

  # ---- examples of chain/layout/format-specific overrides ----
  # Replace/extend these with your real format meanings.
  - match: {layout: StyleHeader, field: format}
    values:
      A: "Format A"
      B: "Format B"

  - match: {chain: "03", layout: StyleHeader, format: A, field: format}
    values:
      A: "Regular Tag (Homegoods)"

input.new_string:
# ---- examples of chain/layout/format-specific overrides ----
  # Replace/extend these with your real meanings. Each block below shows a
  # different matching pattern so you can copy whichever you need.

  # (1) Per-LAYOUT only — same for every chain/format.
  - match: {layout: StyleHeader, field: format}
    values:
      A: "Format A"
      B: "Format B"
      L: "Format L"

  - match: {layout: CartonLabel, field: format}
    values:
      "1": "Carton Format 1"
      "2": "Carton Format 2"

  - match: {layout: Preticket, field: format}
    values:
      L: "Label"
      Y: "Yard"
      N: "Non-Label"

  - match: {layout: DistLabels, field: format}
    values:
      "7": "Distribution 7"
      "9": "Distribution 9"

  # (2) Per-CHAIN + layout — overrides the layout-only rule above for one chain.
  - match: {chain: "03", layout: StyleHeader, field: format}
    values:
      A: "Regular Tag (Homegoods)"
      B: "Sticker (Homegoods)"

  - match: {chain: "01", layout: StyleHeader, field: format}
    values:
      A: "Regular Tag (TJMAXX)"

  # (3) Most specific — chain + layout + FORMAT all pinned.
  - match: {chain: "03", layout: StyleHeader, format: A, field: format}
    values:
      A: "Regular Tag — Compare-at & Up (Homegoods)"

  # (4) Other coded fields (not just `format`).
  - match: {field: type}
    values:
      "1": "Type 1"
      "2": "Type 2"
      "3": "Type 3"

  - match: {layout: StyleHeader, field: indicator}
    values:
      N: "New"
      R: "Reprint"
      C: "Cancel"

  # (5) A field in a repeating section (applies to every row of that section).
  - match: {layout: DistLabels, field: prflag}
    values:
      Y: "Print"
      N: "No Print"

  # (6) Chain-specific label for a detail-section field.
  - match: {chain: "02", layout: CartonLabel, field: prflag}
    values:
      Y: "Print (Marshalls)"
      N: "Suppress (Marshalls)"

content.text:
Done — `config/display.yaml` now has a variety of example rules, all verified to resolve correctly. They demonstrate every matching pattern, ordered from least to most specific:

| # | Pattern | Example rule |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Layout only** | `format` for StyleHeader / CartonLabel / Preticket / DistLabels |
| 2 | **Chain + layout** | `format` `A` → "Regular Tag (Homegoods)" vs "(TJMAXX)" |
| 3 | **Chain + layout + format** (most specific) | chain 03 / StyleHeader / format A |
| 4 | **Other coded fields** | `type`, layout-specific `indicator` (New/Reprint/Cancel) |
| 5 | **Repeating-section field** | `prflag` on every DistLabels row |
| 6 | **Chain-specific detail field** | `prflag` "(Marshalls)" for chain 02 |

Confirmed working: when chain 03 + StyleHeader + format A all match, you get the most-specific label *"Regular Tag — Compare-at & Up (Homegoods)"*; drop the format and it falls back to *"Regular Tag (Homegoods)"*; drop the chain and it's *"Format A"*.

Two reminders:
- **Quote numeric/short codes** (`"1"`, `"7"`) so YAML keeps them as literal characters — I did that in the examples.
- **Restart the server** (Ctrl-C → `okgen serve`, or run with `--debug`) to pick up config edits.

These are placeholder meanings — swap in your real labels whenever you're ready, and tell me if you want me to wire up the actual per-chain `format` codes once you have them. Want me to fold this into the next checkpoint?

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Display-label config: show coded field values as friendly strings.
#
# Each rule maps a field's raw codes to labels. A rule applies to a file when
# its `match` criteria fit. Omit a criterion (or set it to "*") to match any
# value. When several rules match the same field, the MOST SPECIFIC one wins
# (the one matching the most of chain/layout/format).
#
# match keys:
#   chain   2-char chain code ("01".."06"), a LIST like ["03","04"], or "*"  (any)
#   layout  layout name (CartonLabel | DistLabels | Preticket | StyleHeader),
#           a LIST like [StyleHeader, Preticket], or "*"
#   format  the `format` field value (e.g. A, B, L, 1, 7), a LIST, or "*"
#   field   REQUIRED — the output_field_name this rule applies to
#
# Any of chain/layout/format may be a LIST to match several values at once
# (OR). A list still counts as "specific" for tie-breaking, same as a single
# value.
#
# values: { raw_code: "Label shown to user", ... }
#
# Notes:
#   - The `chain` field itself is labeled from chains.yaml; you don't need a
#     rule for it here (but one here would override).
#   - In the editor, a field that has a values map is shown as a dropdown of
#     these labels; saving writes the underlying code at the fixed position.
rules:
  # ---- generic (any chain/layout/format) ----
  - match: {field: indicator}
    values: {Y: "Yes", N: "No"}

  - match: {field: psi}
    values: {Y: "Yes", N: "No"}

  - match: {field: prflag}
    values: {Y: "Yes", N: "No"}

  - match: {field: comp_up}
    values: {Y: "Yes", N: "No"}

  # ---- examples of chain/layout/format-specific overrides ----
  # Replace/extend these with your real meanings. Each block below shows a
  # different matching pattern so you can copy whichever you need.

  # (1) Per-LAYOUT only — same for every chain/format.
  - match: {layout: StyleHeader, field: format}
    values:
      A: "Format A"
      B: "Format B"
      L: "Format L"

  - match: {layout: CartonLabel, field: format}
    values:
      "1": "Carton Format 1"
      "2": "Carton Format 2"

  - match: {layout: Preticket, field: format}
    values:
      L: "Label"
      Y: "Yard"
      N: "Non-Label"

  - match: {layout: DistLabels, field: format}
    values:
      "7": "Distribution 7"
      "9": "Distribution 9"

  # (2) Per-CHAIN + layout — overrides the layout-only rule above for one chain.
  - match: {chain: "03", layout: StyleHeader, field: format}
    values:
      A: "Regular Tag (Homegoods)"
      B: "Sticker (Homegoods)"

  - match: {chain: "01", layout: StyleHeader, field: format}
    values:
      A: "Regular Tag (TJMAXX)"

  # (3) Most specific — chain + layout + FORMAT all pinned.
  - match: {chain: "03", layout: StyleHeader, format: A, field: format}
    values:
      A: "Regular Tag — Compare-at & Up (Homegoods)"

  # (4) Other coded fields (not just `format`).
  - match: {field: type}
    values:
      "1": "Type 1"
      "2": "Type 2"
      "3": "Type 3"

  - match: {layout: StyleHeader, field: indicator}
    values:
      N: "New"
      R: "Reprint"
      C: "Cancel"

  # (5) A field in a repeating section (applies to every row of that section).
  - match: {layout: DistLabels, field: prflag}
    values:
      Y: "Print"
      N: "No Print"

  # (6) Chain-specific label for a detail-section field.
  - match: {chain: "02", layout: CartonLabel, field: prflag}
    values:
      Y: "Print (Marshalls)"
      N: "Suppress (Marshalls)"

"""Tests for the backend service + FastAPI endpoints."""

import os
import shutil
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from okgen.api import service
from okgen.config import Config
from okgen.layout.registry import LayoutRegistry

DATA_DIR = Path(
    os.environ.get(
        "OKGEN_DATA_DIR",
        str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "data" / "OkFileDefinitions"),
    )
)

pytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(
    not DATA_DIR.is_dir(), reason=f"sample data dir not present: {DATA_DIR}"
)


@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def registry():
    return LayoutRegistry.from_dir(DATA_DIR)


@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def config():
    return Config.load()


def test_build_tree_only_ok_files(config):
    tree = service.build_tree(DATA_DIR, config)
    assert tree["type"] == "folder"
    files = [c for c in tree["children"] if c["type"] == "file"]
    assert files, "expected .OK files in tree"
    assert all(f["name"].lower().endswith(".ok") for f in files)
    # No .xlsx leaked into the tree.
    assert not any(f["name"].lower().endswith(".xlsx") for f in files)
    # StyleHeader file carries chain 03 -> Homegoods.
    style = next(f for f in files if f["name"] == "StyleHeader.OK")
    assert style["chain"] == "03"
    assert style["chain_info"]["name"] == "Homegoods"
    assert style["layout"] == "StyleHeader"


def test_parse_file_view_shape(registry, config):
    view = service.parse_file_view(DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK", registry, config)
    assert view["layout"] == "StyleHeader"
    assert view["chain"] == "03"
    names = [s["name"] for s in view["sections"]]
    assert "Lane" in names and "Size" in names
    lane = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Lane")
    assert len(lane["records"]) == 10            # all lanes shown
    assert "lane2" in lane["ignored_fields"]      # unsized fields ignored
    # 'indicator' field carries dropdown options from display.yaml.
    header = view["sections"][0]
    ind = next(f for f in header["fields"] if f["name"] == "indicator")
    assert ind["options"] == {"Y": "Yes", "N": "No"}


def test_save_roundtrip_and_edit(tmp_path, registry, config):
    src = DATA_DIR / "CartonLabel.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "CartonLabel.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    original = work.read_bytes()

    # No-op save must be byte-identical.
    res = service.apply_edits(work, [], registry, backup=False)
    assert res["roundtrip_ok"]
    assert work.read_bytes() == original

    # Edit chain in the header (section 0, record 0).
    res = service.apply_edits(
        work,
        [{"section_index": 0, "record_index": 0, "field": "chain", "value": "07"}],
        registry,
        backup=False,
    )
    assert res["edits_applied"] == 1
    view = service.parse_file_view(work, registry, config)
    assert view["sections"][0]["records"][0]["values"]["chain"] == "07"


def test_save_rejects_too_wide_value(tmp_path, registry):
    src = DATA_DIR / "CartonLabel.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "CartonLabel.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    with pytest.raises(service.EditError):
        service.apply_edits(
            work,
            [{"section_index": 0, "record_index": 0, "field": "chain", "value": "123"}],
            registry,
            backup=False,
        )


def test_save_as_and_copy_delete(tmp_path, registry):
    src = DATA_DIR / "DistLabels.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "DistLabels.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)

    # Save As to a new path.
    other = tmp_path / "DistLabels_copy.OK"
    service.apply_edits(work, [], registry, target_path=str(other), backup=False)
    assert other.exists()

    # Copy + delete file ops.
    dst = tmp_path / "DistLabels_copy2.OK"
    service.copy_file(work, dst)
    assert dst.exists()
    service.delete_file(dst)
    assert not dst.exists()


def test_flask_endpoints():
    from okgen.web.app import create_app

    app = create_app(data_dir=DATA_DIR)
    client = app.test_client()
    assert client.get("/api/health").get_json()["ok"] is True
    chains = client.get("/api/chains").get_json()
    assert chains["03"]["name"] == "Homegoods"
    parsed = client.get(
        "/api/parse", query_string={"path": str(DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK")}
    ).get_json()
    assert parsed["layout"] == "StyleHeader"
    # The HTML UI shell renders.
    assert client.get("/").status_code == 200


def test_max_records_in_view(registry, config):
    view = service.parse_file_view(DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK", registry, config)
    lane = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Lane")
    size = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Size")
    assert lane["max_records"] == 10        # configured limit
    assert size["max_records"] is None      # no limit


def test_add_record_to_unlimited_section(tmp_path, registry, config):
    src = DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "StyleHeader.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    before = service.parse_file_view(work, registry, config)
    size_idx = next(s["index"] for s in before["sections"] if s["name"] == "Size")
    n_before = len(next(s for s in before["sections"] if s["name"] == "Size")["records"])

    view = service.add_record(work, size_idx, [], registry, config, backup=False)
    size = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Size")
    assert len(size["records"]) == n_before + 1
    assert view["roundtrip_ok"]                       # file still well-formed
    # The appended record's fields are blank.
    assert size["records"][-1]["values"]["size"].strip() == ""


def test_add_record_respects_lane_limit(tmp_path, registry, config):
    src = DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK"        # already has 10 lanes (the limit)
    work = tmp_path / "StyleHeader.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    view = service.parse_file_view(work, registry, config)
    lane_idx = next(s["index"] for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Lane")
    with pytest.raises(service.EditError):
        service.add_record(work, lane_idx, [], registry, config, backup=False)


def test_browse_folder_returns_path_key():
    # No GUI in CI: must not raise, and always returns a dict with "path".
    result = service.browse_folder()
    assert "path" in result


def test_flask_save_endpoint(tmp_path):
    import shutil as _sh

    from okgen.web.app import create_app

    src = DATA_DIR / "CartonLabel.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "CartonLabel.OK"
    _sh.copy2(src, work)
    client = create_app(data_dir=DATA_DIR).test_client()
    res = client.post("/api/save", json={
        "path": str(work),
        "edits": [{"section_index": 0, "record_index": 0, "field": "chain", "value": "07"}],
        "backup": False,
    })
    assert res.status_code == 200
    assert res.get_json()["edits_applied"] == 1

content.text:
Yes — done and tested. **38 tests pass.** You can now use a **list** for `chain`, `layout`, or `format` to match several values in one rule. Exactly what you asked for:

```yaml
  - match:
      chain: ["03", "04"]              # chain 03 OR 04
      layout: [StyleHeader, Preticket] # StyleHeader OR Preticket
      field: format
    values:
      A: "Format A"
      B: "Format B"
```

That single rule applies to **chain 03 or 04, in StyleHeader or Preticket**. I added it as example (7) in your `config/display.yaml`.

How it behaves (all verified):
- A list matches if the file's value is **in** the list (OR).
- You can mix: list for `chain`, single value for `layout`, `"*"`/omit for `format`.
- A list still counts as **specific** for tie-breaking, so a single-value rule (e.g. just `chain: "03"`) still wins over a list rule when both match — most-specific always wins.
- Inline `["03","04"]` or block style both work:
  ```yaml
      chain:
        - "03"
        - "04"
  ```

Reminders: **quote numeric codes** (`"04"`, `"1"`) and **restart the server** to load config changes.

There's a fair bit of uncommitted work now (Add-row + folder picker + limits from earlier, plus list-matching and the example rules). Want me to checkpoint it all? I'd tag `phase3d-addrows-folderpicker-listconfig`.

content.text:
Config checkpointed (`94450eb`, tag `config-format-labels`). Now the three fixes.

**1. The "Python quit unexpectedly" crash** — that's the `tkinter` subprocess crashing on macOS (a known Tk-on-Mac issue). I'll replace it with each OS's **native** dialog: AppleScript on Mac, PowerShell on Windows, zenity on Linux. No Python GUI process to crash.

input.old_string:
def browse_folder(initial: Optional[str] = None) -> dict:
    """Open the OS folder-chooser on the local machine and return the choice.

    Runs in a subprocess so Tk never touches the Flask server thread. Works on
    Windows/macOS desktop sessions; returns {"path": None} if cancelled or if
    no GUI is available.
    """
    import subprocess
    import sys

    script = (
        "import tkinter as tk\n"
        "from tkinter import filedialog\n"
        "r = tk.Tk(); r.withdraw(); r.attributes('-topmost', True)\n"
        "print(filedialog.askdirectory() or '')\n"
    )
    try:
        proc = subprocess.run(
            [sys.executable, "-c", script],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=300,
        )
        chosen = proc.stdout.strip()
        return {"path": chosen or None}
    except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - depends on desktop session
        return {"path": None, "error": str(exc)}
input.new_string:
def browse_folder(initial: Optional[str] = None) -> dict:
    """Open the OS-native folder chooser and return the selected path.

    Uses each platform's own dialog (no embedded Python GUI, which can crash):
      * macOS   -> AppleScript ``choose folder`` via ``osascript``
      * Windows -> .NET ``FolderBrowserDialog`` via PowerShell
      * Linux   -> ``zenity --file-selection --directory``

    Returns {"path": None} when cancelled or when no GUI dialog is available.
    """
    import platform
    import subprocess

    system = platform.system()
    try:
        if system == "Darwin":
            prompt = "Select the folder with your OK files"
            script = f'POSIX path of (choose folder with prompt "{prompt}")'
            proc = subprocess.run(
                ["osascript", "-e", script],
                capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=600,
            )
        elif system == "Windows":
            start = (initial or "").replace("'", "''")
            ps = (
                "Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Windows.Forms;"
                "$d = New-Object System.Windows.Forms.FolderBrowserDialog;"
                + (f"$d.SelectedPath = '{start}';" if start else "")
                + "if ($d.ShowDialog() -eq [System.Windows.Forms.DialogResult]::OK)"
                "{ Write-Output $d.SelectedPath }"
            )
            proc = subprocess.run(
                ["powershell", "-NoProfile", "-STA", "-Command", ps],
                capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=600,
            )
        else:
            proc = subprocess.run(
                ["zenity", "--file-selection", "--directory",
                 "--title=Select the folder with your OK files"],
                capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=600,
            )
        chosen = (proc.stdout or "").strip()
        return {"path": chosen or None}
    except FileNotFoundError:
        return {"path": None, "error": "no native folder dialog available on this system"}
    except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover - depends on desktop session
        return {"path": None, "error": str(exc)}

content.text: **2. Add row copies the last row** (not blank), and **3. delete-row** support. Let me update the service.

input.old_string:
def add_record(
    path,
    section_index: int,
    edits: List[dict],
    registry: LayoutRegistry,
    config: Config,
    backup: bool = True,
) -> dict:
    """Apply pending edits, append a blank record to a section, and save.

    Enforces the section's ``max_records`` limit. The new record clones the
    structure (marker, terminator, padding, line ending) of an existing record
    in the section and blanks its field spans, so the file stays well-formed.
    """
    from okgen.okfile import Record

    src = Path(path)
    okf = parse_okfile(src, registry=registry)
    _apply_edits_to_okf(okf, edits)

    grouped = list(okf.sections().items())
    if section_index < 0 or section_index >= len(grouped):
        raise EditError(f"section_index {section_index} out of range")
    sec_name, recs = grouped[section_index]
    if not recs or recs[0].section is None:
        raise EditError(f"section '{sec_name}' has no template record to clone")

    limit = config.max_records(okf.layout.name, sec_name)
    if limit is not None and len(recs) >= limit:
        raise EditError(f"section '{sec_name}' is at its limit of {limit} records")

    template = recs[-1]
    blank = Record(
        raw=_blank_content(template),
        offset=template.offset,
        section=template.section,
        index=template.index,  # placeholder; reassigned on reload
    )
    insert_at = okf.records.index(template) + 1
    okf.records.insert(insert_at, blank)
    _normalize_eols(okf)

    out = src
    if backup and out.exists():
        shutil.copy2(out, out.with_suffix(out.suffix + ".bak"))
    okf.save(out)

    view = parse_file_view(out, registry, config)
    view["added_section"] = sec_name
    return view
input.new_string:
def add_record(
    path,
    section_index: int,
    edits: List[dict],
    registry: LayoutRegistry,
    config: Config,
    backup: bool = True,
) -> dict:
    """Apply pending edits, append a copy of the section's last record, and save.

    The new record duplicates the last existing record of the section (its
    field values included) — users typically copy a row and tweak a few fields.
    Enforces the section's ``max_records`` limit and keeps the file well-formed.
    """
    from okgen.okfile import Record

    src = Path(path)
    okf = parse_okfile(src, registry=registry)
    _apply_edits_to_okf(okf, edits)

    grouped = list(okf.sections().items())
    if section_index < 0 or section_index >= len(grouped):
        raise EditError(f"section_index {section_index} out of range")
    sec_name, recs = grouped[section_index]
    if not recs or recs[0].section is None:
        raise EditError(f"section '{sec_name}' has no record to copy")

    limit = config.max_records(okf.layout.name, sec_name)
    if limit is not None and len(recs) >= limit:
        raise EditError(f"section '{sec_name}' is at its limit of {limit} records")

    template = recs[-1]
    clone = Record(
        raw=template.raw.rstrip("\r"),       # copy values; EOL fixed up below
        offset=template.offset,
        section=template.section,
        index=template.index,                # placeholder; reassigned on reload
    )
    insert_at = okf.records.index(template) + 1
    okf.records.insert(insert_at, clone)
    _normalize_eols(okf)

    _backup_and_save(okf, src, backup)
    view = parse_file_view(src, registry, config)
    view["added_section"] = sec_name
    return view


def delete_record(
    path,
    record_index: int,
    edits: List[dict],
    registry: LayoutRegistry,
    config: Config,
    backup: bool = True,
) -> dict:
    """Apply pending edits, delete one record by its line index, and save.

    The header record (index 0) cannot be deleted.
    """
    src = Path(path)
    okf = parse_okfile(src, registry=registry)
    _apply_edits_to_okf(okf, edits)

    target = next((r for r in okf.records if r.index == record_index), None)
    if target is None:
        raise EditError(f"record_index {record_index} not found")
    if target.index == 0:
        raise EditError("the header record cannot be deleted")

    okf.records.remove(target)
    _normalize_eols(okf)

    _backup_and_save(okf, src, backup)
    return parse_file_view(src, registry, config)


def _backup_and_save(okf, out: Path, backup: bool) -> None:
    if backup and out.exists():
        shutil.copy2(out, out.with_suffix(out.suffix + ".bak"))
    okf.save(out)

def test_add_record_copies_last_row(tmp_path, registry, config):
    src = DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "StyleHeader.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    before = service.parse_file_view(work, registry, config)
    size_sec = next(s for s in before["sections"] if s["name"] == "Size")
    size_idx = size_sec["index"]
    n_before = len(size_sec["records"])
    last_values = size_sec["records"][-1]["values"]

    view = service.add_record(work, size_idx, [], registry, config, backup=False)
    size = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Size")
    assert len(size["records"]) == n_before + 1
    assert view["roundtrip_ok"]                       # file still well-formed
    # The appended record is a copy of the previous last row.
    assert size["records"][-1]["values"] == last_values


def test_delete_record(tmp_path, registry, config):
    src = DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "StyleHeader.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    before = service.parse_file_view(work, registry, config)
    lane = next(s for s in before["sections"] if s["name"] == "Lane")
    n_before = len(lane["records"])
    victim = lane["records"][0]["index"]

    view = service.delete_record(work, victim, [], registry, config, backup=False)
    lane_after = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Lane")
    assert len(lane_after["records"]) == n_before - 1
    assert view["roundtrip_ok"]

"""Tests for the backend service + FastAPI endpoints."""

import os
import shutil
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from okgen.api import service
from okgen.config import Config
from okgen.layout.registry import LayoutRegistry

DATA_DIR = Path(
    os.environ.get(
        "OKGEN_DATA_DIR",
        str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "data" / "OkFileDefinitions"),
    )
)

pytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(
    not DATA_DIR.is_dir(), reason=f"sample data dir not present: {DATA_DIR}"
)


@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def registry():
    return LayoutRegistry.from_dir(DATA_DIR)


FIXTURE_CONFIG = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / "fixtures" / "config"


@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def config():
    return Config.load(FIXTURE_CONFIG)


def test_build_tree_only_ok_files(config):
    tree = service.build_tree(DATA_DIR, config)
    assert tree["type"] == "folder"
    files = [c for c in tree["children"] if c["type"] == "file"]
    assert files, "expected .OK files in tree"
    assert all(f["name"].lower().endswith(".ok") for f in files)
    # No .xlsx leaked into the tree.
    assert not any(f["name"].lower().endswith(".xlsx") for f in files)
    # StyleHeader file carries chain 03 -> Homegoods.
    style = next(f for f in files if f["name"] == "StyleHeader.OK")
    assert style["chain"] == "03"
    assert style["chain_info"]["name"] == "Homegoods"
    assert style["layout"] == "StyleHeader"


def test_parse_file_view_shape(registry, config):
    view = service.parse_file_view(DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK", registry, config)
    assert view["layout"] == "StyleHeader"
    assert view["chain"] == "03"
    names = [s["name"] for s in view["sections"]]
    assert "Lane" in names and "Size" in names
    lane = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Lane")
    assert len(lane["records"]) == 10            # all lanes shown
    assert "lane2" in lane["ignored_fields"]      # unsized fields ignored
    # 'indicator' field carries dropdown options from display.yaml.
    header = view["sections"][0]
    ind = next(f for f in header["fields"] if f["name"] == "indicator")
    assert ind["options"] == {"Y": "Yes", "N": "No"}


def test_save_roundtrip_and_edit(tmp_path, registry, config):
    src = DATA_DIR / "CartonLabel.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "CartonLabel.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    original = work.read_bytes()

    # No-op save must be byte-identical.
    res = service.apply_edits(work, [], registry, backup=False)
    assert res["roundtrip_ok"]
    assert work.read_bytes() == original

    # Edit chain in the header (section 0, record 0).
    res = service.apply_edits(
        work,
        [{"section_index": 0, "record_index": 0, "field": "chain", "value": "07"}],
        registry,
        backup=False,
    )
    assert res["edits_applied"] == 1
    view = service.parse_file_view(work, registry, config)
    assert view["sections"][0]["records"][0]["values"]["chain"] == "07"


def test_save_rejects_too_wide_value(tmp_path, registry):
    src = DATA_DIR / "CartonLabel.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "CartonLabel.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    with pytest.raises(service.EditError):
        service.apply_edits(
            work,
            [{"section_index": 0, "record_index": 0, "field": "chain", "value": "123"}],
            registry,
            backup=False,
        )


def test_save_as_and_copy_delete(tmp_path, registry):
    src = DATA_DIR / "DistLabels.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "DistLabels.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)

    # Save As to a new path.
    other = tmp_path / "DistLabels_copy.OK"
    service.apply_edits(work, [], registry, target_path=str(other), backup=False)
    assert other.exists()

    # Copy + delete file ops.
    dst = tmp_path / "DistLabels_copy2.OK"
    service.copy_file(work, dst)
    assert dst.exists()
    service.delete_file(dst)
    assert not dst.exists()


def test_flask_endpoints():
    from okgen.web.app import create_app

    app = create_app(data_dir=DATA_DIR)
    client = app.test_client()
    assert client.get("/api/health").get_json()["ok"] is True
    chains = client.get("/api/chains").get_json()
    assert chains["03"]["name"] == "Homegoods"
    parsed = client.get(
        "/api/parse", query_string={"path": str(DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK")}
    ).get_json()
    assert parsed["layout"] == "StyleHeader"
    # The HTML UI shell renders.
    assert client.get("/").status_code == 200


def test_max_records_in_view(registry, config):
    view = service.parse_file_view(DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK", registry, config)
    lane = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Lane")
    size = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Size")
    assert lane["max_records"] == 10        # configured limit
    assert size["max_records"] is None      # no limit


def test_add_record_to_unlimited_section(tmp_path, registry, config):
    src = DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "StyleHeader.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    before = service.parse_file_view(work, registry, config)
    size_idx = next(s["index"] for s in before["sections"] if s["name"] == "Size")
    n_before = len(next(s for s in before["sections"] if s["name"] == "Size")["records"])

    view = service.add_record(work, size_idx, [], registry, config, backup=False)
    size = next(s for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Size")
    assert len(size["records"]) == n_before + 1
    assert view["roundtrip_ok"]                       # file still well-formed
    # The appended record's fields are blank.
    assert size["records"][-1]["values"]["size"].strip() == ""


def test_add_record_respects_lane_limit(tmp_path, registry, config):
    src = DATA_DIR / "StyleHeader.OK"        # already has 10 lanes (the limit)
    work = tmp_path / "StyleHeader.OK"
    shutil.copy2(src, work)
    view = service.parse_file_view(work, registry, config)
    lane_idx = next(s["index"] for s in view["sections"] if s["name"] == "Lane")
    with pytest.raises(service.EditError):
        service.add_record(work, lane_idx, [], registry, config, backup=False)


def test_browse_folder_returns_path_key():
    # No GUI in CI: must not raise, and always returns a dict with "path".
    result = service.browse_folder()
    assert "path" in result


def test_flask_save_endpoint(tmp_path):
    import shutil as _sh

    from okgen.web.app import create_app

    src = DATA_DIR / "CartonLabel.OK"
    work = tmp_path / "CartonLabel.OK"
    _sh.copy2(src, work)
    client = create_app(data_dir=DATA_DIR).test_client()
    res = client.post("/api/save", json={
        "path": str(work),
        "edits": [{"section_index": 0, "record_index": 0, "field": "chain", "value": "07"}],
        "backup": False,
    })
    assert res.status_code == 200
    assert res.get_json()["edits_applied"] == 1
